skill.name: threat-mitigation-mapping
skill.description: Map identified threats to appropriate security controls and mitigations. Use when prioritizing security investments, creating remediation plans, or validating control effectiveness.
skill.summary: Connect identified threats to controls, coverage gaps, and practical mitigation roadmaps.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command, C2, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, scanning, and network indicators, but review found them in markdown diagrams, security-control examples, taxonomy labels, and reference links. No prompt injection, malicious execution path, credential access, or data exfiltration was found. A low-risk note remains because the control testing template executes caller-provided test functions if copied into a project.

low_findings.title: Static command execution alerts are false positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are markdown code fences and Python example blocks. They do not invoke a shell or construct commands in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fenced markdown content and Python snippets. No evidence found of shell metacharacters being executed by the skill.

low_findings.title: Security terminology alerts are false positives
low_findings.description: The C2, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and network scanning alerts are caused by security-domain words such as threats, controls, encryption, coverage, DDoS, and control categories. The content maps threats to mitigations and does not provide offensive scanning instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious words appear in defensive control descriptions and STRIDE-style examples. No exploit procedure, target discovery workflow, or command output collection was found.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs do not create network behavior
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are markdown links to NIST, CIS Controls, and MITRE D3FEND resources. They are not runtime requests and do not transmit local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The links are static resource citations at the end of the document. No evidence found of HTTP client code or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Callable test template requires trusted inputs
low_findings.description: The control testing example stores a caller-provided test function and later invokes it. This is not malicious in the skill itself, but copied implementations should only run trusted test functions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly calls test.test_function(), but there is no evidence that the skill supplies malicious functions or fetches untrusted callbacks. The risk depends on downstream use.

content.value_statement: Security teams often struggle to connect identified threats with practical controls and measurable remediation plans. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structure mitigation coverage, gaps, priorities, and roadmaps.
actual_capabilities: Maps threats to preventive, detective, and corrective controls.
Organizes controls across network, application, data, endpoint, process, and physical layers.
Calculates example coverage scores from implementation status and control effectiveness.
Identifies unmapped threats, weak coverage, and missing defense-in-depth layers.
Generates example mitigation reports, recommendations, and phased roadmaps.
limitations: Does not include a live threat intelligence feed or automatic asset discovery.
Requires users to provide accurate threats, controls, costs, and implementation status.
Example scoring logic is simplified and should be calibrated for real risk programs.
Control effectiveness testing requires trusted test functions and local validation.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Remediation Work
use_cases.description: Convert a threat list into control gaps, coverage scores, and an implementation roadmap.

use_cases.title: Review Architecture Coverage
use_cases.description: Check whether proposed designs include layered controls for key threats.

use_cases.title: Prepare Compliance Evidence
use_cases.description: Map controls to frameworks and summarize mitigation status for assessment planning.

prompt_templates.title: Map Basic Threats
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to map these threats to preventive, detective, and corrective controls. Include gaps and simple priorities: [paste threats].

prompt_templates.title: Assess Defense in Depth
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this system design for defense-in-depth coverage. Map each threat to controls by layer and identify missing layers: [paste design and threats].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Remediation Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a phased mitigation roadmap from these threats, existing controls, costs, and implementation statuses: [paste data]. Prioritize critical and high-impact risks.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Control Effectiveness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to define a control effectiveness review plan. Include test objectives, expected evidence, trusted test boundaries, and residual risk notes.

output_examples.input: A payment API faces credential stuffing, injection, and denial-of-service risks.
output_examples.output: The skill would map MFA and account lockout to spoofing risk, input validation and WAF to injection risk, and rate limiting plus DDoS protection to availability risk. It would flag missing detective controls if logging and alerting are absent.

output_examples.input: An architecture has encryption and RBAC but limited monitoring.
output_examples.output: The skill would show strong data and application prevention coverage, then identify weak detective coverage. It would recommend security event logging, log integrity protection, and regular control testing.

output_examples.input: A team needs a quarterly mitigation report for leadership.
output_examples.output: The skill would produce an executive summary with overall risk reduction, critical gaps, defense-in-depth coverage, and a phased roadmap for remaining controls.

best_practices: Provide clear threat names, impact, likelihood, and affected systems before mapping controls.
Use multiple control layers and include preventive, detective, and corrective controls.
Review mappings regularly because threats, implementation status, and control effectiveness change.
anti_patterns: Do not treat one control as complete protection for a high-impact threat.
Do not copy example scores into production without calibrating them to your risk model.
Do not run untrusted control test functions or tests that could disrupt production systems.

faq.question: Does this skill perform vulnerability scanning?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure mitigation mapping and planning from information you provide.

faq.question: Can it replace a formal risk assessment?
faq.answer: No. It supports analysis, but human review is required for final risk decisions.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it reference?
faq.answer: The examples reference common sources such as NIST CSF, CIS Controls, MITRE D3FEND, PCI DSS, OWASP ASVS, and SOC 2.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is written for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What input should I prepare?
faq.answer: Prepare threats, existing controls, control status, approximate costs, and any compliance requirements.

faq.question: Does it send data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found of runtime network requests. The file only includes static reference links.
